name: skill-installer
description: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos).
capabilities: List available curated skills from the OpenAI skills repository
Install skills from curated list by name
Install skills from any GitHub repository by owner/repo/path
Support private repositories using GitHub token authentication
Fallback to git sparse checkout when direct download fails
Validate skill structure and prevent overwriting existing installations
author: openai
securitySummary: This skill installs Codex skills from GitHub repositories with path traversal protection, safe zip extraction, and proper subprocess argument handling. No dangerous patterns detected. Network, filesystem, and git operations are intentional and controlled.
valueStatement: Finding and installing new skills for Codex requires navigating GitHub manually. This skill automates installation from curated lists or any GitHub repository, supporting both public and private repos with proper authentication.
limitations: Only supports GitHub as the skill source repository
Cannot install skills from local files or other version control systems
System skills are preinstalled and cannot be re-installed
GitHub API rate limits may affect listing performance

useCases.title: Explore available skills
useCases.description: Browse the curated skill library to discover new capabilities for your Codex installation.

useCases.title: Install team skills
useCases.description: Deploy custom skills from private team repositories across your organization.

useCases.title: Add custom skills
useCases.description: Install specialized skills from any public GitHub repository to extend Codex functionality.

promptTemplates.title: List available skills
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to see what skills are available
promptTemplates.prompt: What curated skills are available to install?

promptTemplates.title: Install by name
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to install a specific skill
promptTemplates.prompt: Install the skill-1 skill from the curated list.

promptTemplates.title: Install from GitHub
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to install from a specific repo
promptTemplates.prompt: Install the python-helper skill from the github.com/myorg/skills repository.

promptTemplates.title: Private repo install
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to install from a private repo
promptTemplates.prompt: Install the enterprise-skill from github.com/company/private-skills using my GITHUB_TOKEN.

bestPractices: Restart Codex after installing new skills to ensure they are loaded properly
Use GITHUB_TOKEN or GH_TOKEN environment variables for private repository access
Install multiple skills at once by specifying multiple --path arguments
antiPatterns: Do not attempt to overwrite system skills as they are preinstalled
Do not skip the restart step after installation completes
Do not use absolute paths or path traversal sequences in skill names

faq.question: Does this skill work with private GitHub repositories?
faq.answer: Yes. Set the GITHUB_TOKEN or GH_TOKEN environment variable to authenticate with private repos.

faq.question: Where are skills installed on my system?
faq.answer: Skills are installed to $CODEX_HOME/skills, which defaults to ~/.codex/skills.

faq.question: Can I integrate this skill with other automation tools?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the --format json flag for machine-readable output suitable for scripts and pipelines.

faq.question: Is my code safe when installing skills from GitHub?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill validates paths and extracts archives safely to prevent directory traversal attacks.

faq.question: What should I do if installation fails?
faq.answer: Check that the repository exists, the path is correct, and you have proper authentication for private repos.

faq.question: How is this different from manual skill installation?
faq.answer: This skill automates the entire process including authentication handling, git fallback, and installation validation.
